title: Method that does not use instance state should be static
summary: A method that never touches this, an instance field, or an instance member does not depend on the object it is called on. Leaving it as an instance method hides that fact, forces callers to hold an instance they do not need, and adds an avoidable this argument to every call.
Mark the method static to state that it is independent of instance state. Members that implement an interface or override a base member are excluded.
messageFormat: Make this method static; it does not use any instance state.
